extra.message: The `name` variable, sourced directly from `request.args.get('name')` in sandboxes/views.py:13, is rendered with the `| safe` Jinja2 filter. This bypasses Jinja2's automatic HTML escaping and allows an attacker to inject arbitrary HTML/JavaScript via the `name` GET parameter (e.g., `?name=<script>alert(1)</script>`), resulting in reflected XSS.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: The `name` variable, sourced from `request.cookies.get('name')` or `request.form.get('name')` in sandboxes/views.py:27-34, is rendered with the `| safe` Jinja2 filter. This disables HTML escaping and allows an attacker to inject arbitrary HTML/JavaScript through a crafted `name` cookie or POST form field, resulting in stored/reflected XSS.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: The `message.message` field is rendered with `| safe`, bypassing HTML escaping. This field is populated from unauthenticated POST requests to `/api/v1/messages` (api/views.py:43-56) where any user can submit a message. An attacker can store a malicious payload which is then executed in the admin's browser when viewing the messages panel, resulting in stored XSS targeting admin users.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: The `message.from_user` field is rendered with `| safe`, bypassing HTML escaping. This field is populated from unauthenticated POST requests to `/api/v1/messages` (api/views.py:50) where any user can supply an arbitrary `from_user` string. An attacker can store a malicious payload which executes in the admin's browser, resulting in stored XSS targeting admin users.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: The `g.user.username` field is rendered with `| safe` in the navigation bar, bypassing HTML escaping. If a user registers with a username containing HTML/JavaScript (e.g., `<script>alert(1)</script>`), it will be executed in every page the user visits while logged in, resulting in persistent XSS.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: The `password` field in the `User` model is stored and compared as plaintext (models.py:11, login/views.py:17: `user.password == password`). No hashing or salting is applied. If the database is compromised, all user passwords are immediately exposed. Additionally, the `serialize` property (models.py:27) includes the plaintext password in API responses.

cwe: CWE-256: Plaintext Storage of a Password

extra.message: The `serialize` property on the `User` model includes the `password` field (plaintext). This is returned in API responses via the `/api/v1/users` endpoint (api/views.py:72), exposing all user passwords to the admin. Combined with plaintext storage, this is a direct credential disclosure.

cwe: CWE-200: Exposure of Sensitive Information to an Unauthorized Actor

extra.message: The `next` parameter is taken directly from `request.args.get('next')` and passed to `redirect()` without any validation. An attacker can craft a URL like `/toggle_xss_protection/?next=https://evil.com` to redirect users to an arbitrary external site, facilitating phishing attacks.

cwe: CWE-601: URL Redirection to Untrusted Site ('Open Redirect')

extra.message: The Flask `SECRET_KEY` is hardcoded as the string `'Keep it secret, keep it safe'`. This key is used to sign session cookies. Anyone with knowledge of this key can forge session cookies and impersonate any user, including the admin.

Flask's `DEBUG` mode is hardcoded to `True`. In production this enables the interactive Werkzeug debugger which allows arbitrary Python code execution via a browser when an unhandled exception occurs. It also exposes detailed tracebacks with sensitive application internals.

cwe: CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code ('Code Injection')

extra.message: The `after_request` hook explicitly sets `X-XSS-Protection: 0` on every response by default (when `xss_protection_enabled` is False, which is the default). This disables the browser's built-in XSS filter for all users unless they explicitly toggle it on, increasing exploitability of the XSS vulnerabilities present in the application.

The `MessagesView.post()` method (POST `/api/v1/messages`) has no authentication requirement. Any unauthenticated user can submit arbitrary messages with any `from_user` value. Combined with the stored XSS via `| safe` in the admin message template, this creates a fully unauthenticated stored XSS attack chain against admin users.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: The hidden input `value={{ g.path }}` on line 67 is not quoted in the HTML attribute context. `g.path` contains `request.path` set in `before_request`. While Flask's Jinja2 auto-escaping applies here (no `| safe`), the lack of quotes means an attacker controlling path characters (e.g., spaces) could break out of the attribute context in some edge cases. More critically, `g.path` is also unquoted in the `action={{ url_for('xss_protection.index') }}` and `src={{ url_for("static", filename="test.js") }}` attributes on lines 66 and 79, meaning any space in those values would break the HTML attribute parsing.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')
